meta_title: Inflatable screen for drive-in cinema — Hallucine
meta_desc: The Hallucine inflatable screen designed for drive-in cinema: raised image base at 3 m, permanent blower, setup in 30 minutes.
hero_title: The inflatable screen designed for drive-in
hero_desc: Showing a film to an audience in cars brings a unique constraint: everyone must see the screen, all the way to the back rows. Hallucine builds a dedicated screen, with a raised image base.
why_title: Why a screen built for drive-in?
why_p1: At a drive-in cinema, cars park in successive rows facing the screen. With a standard screen whose image starts 1.6 to 2.2 m off the ground, vehicles in the back rows have their view blocked by the cars in front.
why_p2: The Hallucine Drive-In screen raises the image base to 3 metres. The image clears the rooftops: every viewer, from the first to the last row, enjoys a full, comfortable picture.
stat_desc: Height between the ground and the bottom of the projected image — above the cars' rooftops.
card_desc: A permanent-blower model, sized and raised for cinema in cars.
prix_caption: Price excl. VAT — 2026 rates, delivery included.
audio_note: For sound, Hallucine offers an FM transmitter: each viewer tunes their car radio to the screening's frequency.
a1_desc: The 3 m image base clears the view above the cars, all the way to the last row.
a2_desc: The screen stays perfectly taut and stable throughout the screening.
a3_desc: Guyed structure designed for outdoor projection, a whole evening long.
a4_desc: Fast installation, with no complex tooling, for a hassle-free on-site deployment.
cta_desc: Tell us about your event — capacity, location, dates. We'll advise the right screen and prepare a quote.
photo_alt: A giant Hallucine inflatable cinema screen set up outdoors, with cars parked nearby
card_photo_alt: 17-metre Hallucine inflatable cinema screen installed for a drive-in cinema — large-format outdoor projection
